# Number Theory

## Topic-Specific Variants

- Non-coprime CRT systems that are compatible until one congruence changes.
- Cases indistinguishable after partial normalization but separated by gcd.
- Prime-power inputs where square-free or prime-only reasoning gives the same small samples.
- Divisor-shape counterexamples: same size scale, different divisor lattice.

## Strong Adversarial Solutions

- Coprime CRT logic extends to non-coprime moduli.
- Modular inverse or division is valid after normalization alone.
- Square-free behavior represents repeated-prime-power behavior.
- Random-looking numbers cover the divisor shapes that control the answer.

## Killer Constructions

- Pair coprime and non-coprime instances with similar residues.
- Build CRT systems that differ by one congruence becoming incompatible.
- Replace primes by prime powers while keeping superficial magnitude similar.
- Construct two numbers with similar size and very different divisor lattices.
